entries.name: Burnol / Silverex
entries.contraindications: Deep, severe 3rd degree burns. Stop if skin irritation occurs.
entries.season_warnings: Essential in Chilla Kalan due to frequent Kangri (fire pot) burns.
entries.text_content: Burnol is a critical first-aid cream in Kashmiri households during the winter. It provides immediate cooling relief and prevents infection on superficial Kangri burns or hot liquid scalds.

entries.name: Volini / Moov Spray
entries.contraindications: Do not apply on broken skin or open wounds.
entries.season_warnings: Highly used in extreme winter when slipping on icy roads (Frost) is common.
entries.text_content: Pain relief sprays are essential for immediate relief from muscle sprains, which are very common in Kashmir during winter due to slippery, icy roads.

entries.name: Betadine Ointment
entries.usage: Antiseptic for minor cuts, scrapes, and wounds.
entries.contraindications: Thyroid disorders (if used extensively over large areas).
entries.text_content: Povidone-iodine (Betadine) is the standard antiseptic ointment. Apply to small cuts to prevent infection before bandaging.

entries.name: Crepe Bandage
entries.contraindications: Do not wrap too tightly (can cut off circulation).
entries.text_content: Provides firm support and compression to injured ankles or wrists. Very common in winter due to falls on snow/ice.

entries.name: ORS (Oresol / Electral)
entries.usage: Dehydration due to diarrhea or vomiting.
entries.text_content: Oral Rehydration Salts are essential for restoring lost fluids and electrolytes during bouts of diarrhea or vomiting, especially common in summer.
